title: Persistence — Character Database
description: Create a MUDDatabase using sqlite3. Tables: characters (name TEXT PRIMARY KEY, class TEXT, level INTEGER, health INTEGER, max_health INTEGER, attack INTEGER, defense INTEGER, experience INTEGER, gold INTEGER, room TEXT) and inventory (char_name TEXT, item_name TEXT, quantity INTEGER). Methods: save_character(character, room_name), load_character(name), delete_character(name), list_characters(). Auto-create tables on init. Use context managers for connections.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 8: MUD Database
import os
import tempfile

assert 'MUDDatabase' in dir(), 'MUDDatabase class must be defined'

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
    db_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'mud_test.db')
    db = MUDDatabase(db_path)

    # Test database file was created
    assert os.path.exists(db_path), 'Database file should be created on init'

    # Test save and load character
    char = Character('Hero', 'warrior', 120, 15, 12)
    char.gold = 500
    char.experience = 250
    char.level = 3

    db.save_character(char, 'tavern')
    loaded = db.load_character('Hero')
    assert loaded is not None, 'Should load saved character'
    assert isinstance(loaded, dict), 'load_character should return a dict'
    assert loaded['name'] == 'Hero', f'Name should be Hero, got {loaded["name"]}'
    assert loaded['class'] == 'warrior', f'Class should be warrior, got {loaded["class"]}'
    assert loaded['level'] == 3, f'Level should be 3, got {loaded["level"]}'
    assert loaded['gold'] == 500, f'Gold should be 500, got {loaded["gold"]}'
    assert loaded['room'] == 'tavern', f'Room should be tavern, got {loaded["room"]}'
    assert loaded['experience'] == 250, f'Experience should be 250, got {loaded["experience"]}'

    # Test load nonexistent character
    assert db.load_character('Ghost') is None, 'Should return None for unknown character'

    # Test update character
    char.gold = 1000
    db.save_character(char, 'market')
    updated = db.load_character('Hero')
    assert updated['gold'] == 1000, f'Gold should be updated to 1000, got {updated["gold"]}'
    assert updated['room'] == 'market', f'Room should be updated to market, got {updated["room"]}'

    # Test list_characters
    char2 = Character('Mage', 'mage', 80, 20, 8)
    db.save_character(char2)
    names = db.list_characters()
    assert isinstance(names, list), 'list_characters should return a list'
    assert sorted(names) == ['Hero', 'Mage'], f'Should list both characters, got {names}'

    # Test delete_character
    db.delete_character('Mage')
    assert db.load_character('Mage') is None, 'Mage should be gone after delete'
    assert 'Mage' not in db.list_characters(), 'Mage should not be in list after delete'

    # Test inventory
    db.save_inventory('Hero', [('iron sword', 1), ('health potion', 5)])
    inv = db.load_inventory('Hero')
    assert isinstance(inv, list), 'load_inventory should return a list'
    assert len(inv) == 2, f'Should have 2 inventory items, got {len(inv)}'
    inv_dict = {name: qty for name, qty in inv}
    assert inv_dict.get('iron sword') == 1, f'Should have 1 iron sword, got {inv_dict}'
    assert inv_dict.get('health potion') == 5, f'Should have 5 health potions, got {inv_dict}'

    # Test inventory overwrite
    db.save_inventory('Hero', [('iron sword', 2)])
    inv2 = db.load_inventory('Hero')
    assert len(inv2) == 1, f'Inventory should be replaced, got {len(inv2)} items'
    assert inv2[0] == ('iron sword', 2), f'Should have 2 iron swords, got {inv2}'

    # Test delete character also deletes inventory
    db.delete_character('Hero')
    inv3 = db.load_inventory('Hero')
    assert len(inv3) == 0, 'Inventory should be deleted with character'
acceptance_criteria: MUDDatabase creates SQLite file and tables on init
save_character() inserts or updates character data with room name
load_character() returns a dict of character data or None
delete_character() removes character and their inventory
list_characters() returns a list of all character names
save_inventory() replaces a character's inventory items
load_inventory() returns list of (item_name, quantity) tuples
All database access uses context managers
hints: Use 'INSERT OR REPLACE INTO characters (name, class, level, ...) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ...)' for upsert.
Wrap DB access in: with self._connect() as conn: conn.execute(...)
For load_character, use conn.execute(...).fetchone() and map columns to a dict.
In delete_character, delete from both 'inventory' and 'characters' tables.
For save_inventory, DELETE WHERE char_name=? first, then INSERT each item.
